Campus

The whole town is our campus.

Kamiyama is a town of about 5,000 people in Tokushima — a unique place where art and IT coexist with the mountains. The school buildings, dorms, town facilities and nature itself shape five years of learning.

Gallery

Fragments of a place

Images are illustrative — courtesy of Unsplash photographers.

Facilities

Main facilities

  • Studios & classrooms

    Open spaces for making, prototyping and dialogue.

  • Dormitory

    A residential life that becomes a second classroom.

  • Dining & cafeteria

    Local ingredients, conversations born around the table.

  • Town facilities & satellite offices

    Working with offices and workshops scattered across town.

Kamiyama Marugoto College of Technology

Kamiyama Marugoto College of Technology

An unofficial introduction to Kamiyama Marugoto College of Technology, a private KOSEN located in Kamiyama, Tokushima, Japan.
